zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow於大約1240年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾盧德洛福時，他的父親Matthew LUDLOW, de Ludlowe, Ludlow61歲，母親Petronilla Swineton, de Swinton31歲。他和Margery De Ludlow of Deverill至少有3個兒子。他去世於大約1300年在他的家鄉享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾盧德洛福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ludlowe,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from the Old English river name Hlūde (from hlūd ‘loud, roaring’) referring to the Teme river + hlāw ‘hill’. This name was taken Ireland in the 13th and 17th centuries. See also Laidlaw .

variants.name: Laidlaw
meanings: Scottish: Border surname found mainly around Selkirk and the vales of Ettrick and Yarrow; a Scottish development of   Ludlow  .

variants.name: Ledlow
meanings: English, Irish, or Scottish: variant of   Ludlow   or   Laidlaw  .

variants.name: Ludlam
meanings: English: possibly from a misreading of w as m in Ludelawe, a recorded medieval spelling of the placename Ludlow in Shropshire. Compare Ludlow .

nameMeanings.name: Ludlow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from the Old English river name Hlūde (from hlūd ‘loud, roaring’) referring to the Teme river + hlāw ‘hill’. This name was taken Ireland in the 13th and 17th centuries. See also Laidlaw .

variants.name: Laidlaw
meanings: Scottish: Border surname found mainly around Selkirk and the vales of Ettrick and Yarrow; a Scottish development of   Ludlow  .

variants.name: Ledlow
meanings: English, Irish, or Scottish: variant of   Ludlow   or   Laidlaw  .

variants.name: Ludlam
meanings: English: possibly from a misreading of w as m in Ludelawe, a recorded medieval spelling of the placename Ludlow in Shropshire. Compare Ludlow .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow was born about 1240, in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Matthew LUDLOW, de Ludlowe, Ludlow, was 61 and his mother, Petronilla Swineton, de Swinton, was 31. He had at least 3 sons with Margery De Ludlow of Deverill. He died about 1300, in his hometown, at the age of 62, and was buried in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow est né environ 1240, à Ludlow, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Matthew LUDLOW, de Ludlowe, Ludlow, avait 61 ans et sa mère, Petronilla Swineton, de Swinton, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Margery De Ludlow of Deverill. Il est décédé environ 1300, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Ludlow, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hatte mit Margery De Ludlow of Deverill mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb um 1300 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow(이)가 대략 1240년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 루들로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Matthew LUDLOW, de Ludlowe, Ludlow(은)는 61세였고, 그의 어머니 Petronilla Swineton, de Swinton(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Margery De Ludlow of Deverill(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1300년 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 루들로에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas de Ludlowe, Ludlow родился около 1240 г. в местности лудлоу, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Matthew LUDLOW, de Ludlowe, Ludlow было 61 год, а матери, которую звали Petronilla Swineton, de Swinton, 31. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Margery De Ludlow of Deverill. Он умер около 1300 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности лудлоу, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.
